message:
fix(cursor): resolve vanishing custom cursor over technical content areas

The custom cursor (green ring + dot) would vanish when hovering over
<pre>/<code> elements—most noticeably on the architecture page's cell.rs
diagram area. Three interacting causes:

1. AnimatePresence fragment re-keying: The outer AnimatePresence wrapped a
   React fragment whose children changed count/order when isTechnicalArea
   toggled (DataDebris appeared, shifting ring/dot positions in the
   fragment). Without explicit keys, AnimatePresence misidentified which
   children were entering vs exiting, triggering exit animations on the
   ring and dot—making them disappear.

2. mix-blend-screen on green-over-black: The outer ring used
   mix-blend-screen, which made it nearly invisible over the green-on-
   black code areas. Removed the blend mode so the ring renders with
   consistent opacity regardless of background content.

3. Incomplete cursor:none coverage: The global CSS only targeted body and
   interactive elements (a, button, etc.) via inheritance. Elements with
   their own cursor styles (pre with cursor:text from user-agent
   stylesheets, overflow:auto containers) would show system cursors,
   breaking the illusion. Now uses `*, *::before, *::after` universal
   selector for complete coverage.

Structural fix:
- Flashlight and DataDebris effects render independently (not inside
  AnimatePresence), since they don't need enter/exit animations.
- Core cursor elements (ring + dot) render as simple conditional block
  without AnimatePresence wrapping—they appear/disappear instantly with
  isVisible, no animation needed.
- Crosshairs get their own AnimatePresence with explicit key="crosshair"
  for clean enter/exit transitions.
- Removed overflow-hidden from cursor container (was unnecessary and
  could clip at viewport edges).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
